what_it_does: Records metadata for a single image attachment in the design-source channel, linking it to an image resource at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-13.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp.
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/deck1-2026/p-13.
File has no body content beyond the frontmatter.
standouts: Uses a custom image:// URI scheme for the target.
Timestamp includes microsecond precision and timezone offset.
conventions: id uses att- prefix with a short hash.
References external media via a URI rather than embedding it.
concerns: No descriptive title, caption, or alt text for the image.
Target URI validity cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: This is a minimal attachment ledger entry that points to a slide/page image in a 2026 deck.
questions: What does p-13 represent within deck1-2026?
Is image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-13 resolvable by the system?
Are there other attachment types besides images?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment ledger entry; its content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: To catalog an image attachment associated with the design-source channel so it can be retrieved or referenced in context.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to address channel-local media assets.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment record that links a channel to an external image resource.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry for an image in the design-source channel, targeting image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-13 and recording its addition time.
intention_for_embedding: To register and locate a specific image asset attached to the design-source channel.
